summary:
This bill provides fiscal year 2025 appropriations for energy and water development and related agencies through September 30, 2025, with many funds remaining available until expended or through September 30, 2026. It funds the Army Corps of Engineers civil works programs, including investigations, construction, Mississippi River and tributaries, operation and maintenance, regulatory activities, cleanup of formerly used atomic energy sites, and flood and coastal emergencies. It funds the Department of the Interior’s Central Utah Project and Bureau of Reclamation water programs, including Central Valley Project restoration and California Bay-Delta restoration.

The bill funds Department of Energy programs for energy efficiency and renewable energy, cybersecurity and energy security, electricity, grid deployment, nuclear energy, fossil energy and carbon management, petroleum reserves, the Energy Information Administration, environmental cleanup, science, nuclear waste disposal, technology transitions, ARPA-E, loan program administration, Indian energy, departmental administration, and the Inspector General. It also funds the National Nuclear Security Administration’s weapons, nonproliferation, naval reactors, and federal salaries accounts; defense environmental cleanup and other defense activities; power marketing administrations; the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission; regional commissions; the Nuclear Regulatory Commission; the Defense Nuclear Facilities Safety Board; and the Nuclear Waste Technical Review Board.

The bill sets reprogramming, transfer, notification, reporting, and work plan requirements for funded agencies. It restricts certain uses of funds, including for specified Corps and Reclamation activities, certain DOE rules and initiatives, Strategic Petroleum Reserve sales involving China, awards to “entities of concern,” certain procurements involving Chinese ownership, COVID-19 mask or vaccine mandates, Critical Race Theory-related activities, specified equity executive orders, and certain energy conservation rules. It requires guidance documents on the 2023 “waters of the United States” rule within 15 days and a Corps baseline report within 60 days.

The bill amends existing law by extending and increasing authorizations related to CALFED, drought relief, water recycling and desalination, modifying WIIN Act consultation provisions, changing Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act eligibility language, and amending the Natural Gas Act to give FERC exclusive authority over approvals for natural gas import and export facilities while preserving presidential sanctions and emergency authorities. It also transfers specified unobligated balances to nuclear energy projects and reduces certain Inflation Reduction Act funding amounts.
